name: PowerShell Encoded Command Execution (T1059.001, T1140)
description: Detects PowerShell launched with -EncodedCommand, -enc, or -ec parameters used to pass obfuscated base64-encoded commands. Commonly combined with -ExecutionPolicy Bypass and -WindowStyle Hidden for stealthy execution. Captures both the base64 argument pattern and suspicious parameter combinations.
query: process where event.type == "start" and process.name : ("powershell.exe", "pwsh.exe") and process.args : ("-EncodedCommand", "-encodedcommand", "-enc", "-ec") and not process.parent.name : ("ccmexec.exe", "sccmexec.exe")
author: F0RT1KA Detection Rules Generator

name: PowerShell Hidden Window with Execution Policy Bypass (T1059.001)
description: Detects powershell.exe or pwsh.exe launched with both -WindowStyle Hidden and -ExecutionPolicy Bypass flags. This specific argument combination is characteristic of attacker tradecraft to silently execute scripts without user visibility. The hidden window prevents user notification while the bypass removes execution restrictions.
query: process where event.type == "start" and process.name : ("powershell.exe", "pwsh.exe") and process.command_line : ("*-WindowStyle Hidden*", "*-w hidden*", "*-wi h*") and process.command_line : ("*-ExecutionPolicy Bypass*", "*-ep bypass*", "*-ep b*")
author: F0RT1KA Detection Rules Generator

name: PowerShell Download Cradle with In-Memory Execution (T1059.001)
description: Detects PowerShell download cradle patterns that combine a download method (Net.WebClient, Invoke-WebRequest) with immediate in-memory execution via IEX or Invoke-Expression. This fileless delivery technique fetches and executes remote code without writing a payload to disk, bypassing file-based detection.
query: process where event.type == "start" and process.name : ("powershell.exe", "pwsh.exe") and process.command_line : ("*Net.WebClient*", "*WebClient*", "*Invoke-WebRequest*", "*DownloadString*", "*DownloadFile*") and process.command_line : ("*IEX*", "*Invoke-Expression*", "*| iex*")
author: F0RT1KA Detection Rules Generator

name: AMSI Bypass Pattern Detected in PowerShell Script Block (T1059.001)
description: Detects AMSI bypass indicator strings captured by Windows PowerShell Script Block Logging (Event ID 4104). Script Block Logging records the decoded content of executed scripts, catching obfuscated AMSI bypass code after decoding. The listed strings (AmsiScanBuffer, amsiInitFailed, AmsiUtils) are characteristic of the most prevalent AMSI bypass technique families.
author: F0RT1KA Detection Rules Generator

name: PowerShell Multi-Stage Attack Chain Sequence (T1059.001, T1140)
description: Uses an EQL sequence to detect a multi-step PowerShell attack chain: encoded command execution followed by file creation within 60 seconds on the same host. This sequence is characteristic of attackers delivering an encoded first stage that then drops additional tooling or establishes persistence. The sequence correlation significantly reduces false positive rate compared to individual indicator alerting.
query:
sequence by host.id with maxspan=60s
  [process where event.type == "start" and process.name : ("powershell.exe", "pwsh.exe") and process.args : ("-EncodedCommand", "-enc", "-ec") and process.command_line : ("*-ExecutionPolicy Bypass*", "*-ep bypass*")]
  [file where event.type == "creation" and process.name : ("powershell.exe", "pwsh.exe") and not file.path : ("*\\Windows\\Temp\\*", "*\\AppData\\Local\\Temp\\*")]
author: F0RT1KA Detection Rules Generator
